regime: Risk-On Day 2 (NQ Fresh Highs, Commodities Whipsaw)
summary: Day 2 of the risk-on, and now it is squarely an equity story. MNQ extended Monday's reversal into a clean trend day: it ran through the 13:00 hour, broke its opening range UP at 13:50 UTC and held, then ground to a fresh 30,600 RTH high before settling 30,501 (+1.69%). MES confirmed, +0.65% to 7,541.75, holding above the 7,500 breakout on every hourly close (RTH low 7,515.5). Both TSMOM-20 legs sit above rising 1hr stops, with NQ leading ES roughly three to one. The commodity side decoupled into whipsaw: gold flipped its 1hr stop back long overnight, then failed a 4,106 pop and closed 4,050.5 (-0.21%) heavy near the RTH low, stretched 2.3 sigma below its session VWAP; crude lost its stop midsession and flipped short (-0.58%, 70.04). The easy money was Monday's reversal; today is extension into round-number resistance, and it prints into quarter-end markup ahead of a holiday-shortened jobs week (ADP and ISM Wed, June jobs Thu, market closed Fri).

keyLevels.label: MGC ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: Flipped long; fragile support after a failed pop

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (LONG, healthy)
setups.entry: Already long; the system stays long while hourly closes hold above the 30,242 stop
setups.t1: Hold 30,500, then the +2s 30,628 and fresh highs above 30,600
setups.bias: In-habitat trend long at fresh highs, but Day 2 into quarter-end and the jobs week. No chase at 30,500 / 30,600; let pullbacks toward AVWAP 30,484 define risk. No adds, no early exit; the stop decides.

setups.name: ES Alignment Watch (MES)
setups.entry: Holds above 7,500 on hourly closes keeps the equity complex aligned long
setups.bias: MES held 7,500 all session (RTH low 7,515.5) and confirmed NQ. A loss of the 7,515 stop turns ES the laggard and warns the NQ long.

event.name: No first-tier US release (quarter-end Tuesday; second-tier Chicago PMI and CB Consumer Confidence). The data cluster is Jul 1 to Jul 2.
event.consensus: Quiet first-tier slate. The focus was quarter-end positioning and the holiday-shortened jobs week (ADP and ISM Wed, June jobs Thu), not a single print.
event.actual: Second-tier only: MNI Chicago PMI and CB Consumer Confidence. Price action was Day 2 of the risk-on, led by mega-cap tech.
event.surprise: None first-tier. The move was momentum and quarter-end markup: a clean gap-up-and-go that held its opening-range break.
event.nqReaction: MNQ broke its opening range UP at 13:50 UTC (30,294) and held, trending to a fresh 30,600 RTH high before settling 30,501 (+1.69%). It never pulled back to the OR low.
event.crossAsset: MES +0.65% to 7,541.75 (held 7,500 all session). MGC -0.21% to 4,050.5, flipped its 1hr stop back long overnight but failed a 4,106 pop and closed heavy near lows. MCL -0.58% to 70.04, flipped its 1hr stop short midsession. Risk-on is equity-led; commodities whipsawed.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: No gap-fade setup; the tape gapped up and extended into a one-directional trend day. The fade is invalid in a quarter-end markup.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: The longer-horizon long is firmly intact at fresh highs, well clear of the short-horizon 30,242 stop.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES held the 7,500 breakout on every hourly close (RTH low 7,515.5), the cleanest both-aligned read since the PCE scare.

risk.throttleState: REDUCED (held, NOT upgraded to NORMAL). TSMOM-20 is a healthy ES-confirmed long at fresh highs, +259 over a rising stop, but Day 2 of a vertical move into round-number resistance, quarter-end markup, and the jobs week argue against full size.
risk.ddProximity: Comfortable. The 259-pt MNQ buffer above the stop is healthy and drawdown proximity is low. The risk is not the stop, it is giving back open profit by chasing.
risk.dailyLossBudget: Intact. The binding risk is overtrading a thin quarter-end and holiday tape and chasing extension into the jobs print, not a gap to the stop.
risk.sizing: Hold REDUCED; do not upgrade to NORMAL into quarter-end and the jobs week. Respect the 30,242 stop; do not chase 30,500 / fresh highs; no adds into the markup. The long-only equity stack takes neither the gold flip nor the crude short.
risk.note: Week ahead (holiday-shortened): Wed Jul 1 ADP 8:15 ET and ISM Manufacturing 10:00 ET (Q3 start); Thu Jul 2 June jobs report 8:30 ET then a 1:00 pm ET early close; Fri Jul 3 market CLOSED (Independence Day observed; Jul 4 Saturday).

psychology: Day 2 is where confidence quietly turns into carelessness. The reversal was Monday's trade; today was the easy continuation; and after two green days the temptation is to size up because the highs feel safe. That instinct is backwards. The edge was the failed breakdown forty-eight hours ago, when it felt dangerous. Chasing 30,500 on Day 2, into quarter-end markup and a holiday-thin jobs week, is exactly where a good run gives its gains back. The professional move is unglamorous: trail the winner on the 30,242 stop, resist adding into a vertical move, and let the market come to your level. You do not have to catch every point of a trend to be paid by it.

fullReport.title: Market Regime Snapshot (6-Axis)
fullReport.html:
AxisStateConfidence
DirectionRisk-ON, equity-led uptrend, DAY 2. MNQ +1.69% to 30,501 printed fresh RTH highs (30,600), +259 over a RISING TSMOM-20 stop; MES +0.65% to 7,541.75 held above 7,500, also long and rising. NQ leads ES ~3x. Gold flipped its 1hr stop back LONG overnight but FADED to close heavy (-0.21%); crude flipped SHORT (-0.58%).HIGH
VolatilityCompression after expansion. MNQ ATR(14) 118 (down from Monday's 150). RTH range 30,274 to 30,600 (~326 pts) with price held high. An orderly markup, not a volatile trend day.MODERATE
SessionGap-up-and-go then high consolidation. MNQ ran in the 13:00 hour, broke its OR UP at 13:50 UTC and HELD, grinding to 30,600 then settling near 30,500. The mirror of Monday's failed-breakdown squeeze.HIGH
InventoryNet long, comfortable. Buyers in control from the open; price never revisited the OR low. Quarter-end markup into the close.MODERATE
EventPOST/PRE. Today: quarter-end (Chicago PMI, CB Consumer Confidence, secondary). Ahead: Wed Jul 1 ADP 8:15 ET + ISM Manufacturing 10:00 ET (Q3 start); Thu Jul 2 June jobs 8:30 ET then 1pm ET early close; Fri Jul 3 market CLOSED.HIGH
LiquidityThin and thinning. Quarter-end rebalancing today; holiday-shortened week ahead (half-day Thu, closed Fri). Markup tape can distort; size down.MODERATE

Cross-asset: Day 2 of the risk-on, now narrowly equity-led. NQ printed fresh highs above 30,500, ES held the 7,500 breakout, both stops ratcheting up. The commodity complex decoupled into whipsaw: gold flipped its 1hr stop back long overnight then failed a 4,106 pop, closing heavy and stretched 2.3 sigma below its session VWAP; crude flipped short after losing its stop midsession. Risk appetite is concentrated in equities, not broad commodity reflation.

fullReport.title: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ)
fullReport.html: NY Opening Range / COMPASS (MNQ). OR (13:30 to 13:44 UTC) 30,034 to 30,264, width 230 (wide, reflecting the gap-up open). Break UP at 13:50 UTC (close 30,294 above the OR high) that HELD: price never returned to the OR low and trended to a 30,600 RTH high. MES showed the same upside-break-and-hold (OR break up 13:50). The clean, held breakout was the mirror of Monday's failed-breakdown squeeze, and the COMPASS tell of a genuine trend day.

fullReport.title: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter)
fullReport.html: Momentum Breakout (Regime Filter). Long confirmed and sustained. Price held above AVWAP all session with higher highs and a held opening-range break; the regime filter stayed constructive into the close. The caveat is maturity: this is Day 2 of the move into round-number resistance (30,500 / 30,600), and ATR(14) compressed to 118 from Monday's 150, so the trend is orderly but no longer fresh. Do not extrapolate a quarter-end markup into the thin holiday tape.

fullReport.title: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ)
fullReport.html: AVWAP Deviation Band Reversal (MNQ). Center 30,484, sigma 72. Bands: +1s 30,556, +2s 30,628, +3s 30,700; -1s 30,412, -2s 30,341, -3s 30,269. Price closed 30,501, just above AVWAP and inside the +1s band, extended but not stretched to a reversion trigger. The compressed sigma (72 vs Monday's 159) reflects an orderly markup rather than a volatile trend. Re-qualifies for a fade only on a clean +2s/-2s tag with an exhaustion bar and a center reclaim, half size. AVWAP/bands are price-based this run (volume unavailable; Alphatrends indicator not loaded), disclaimed accordingly.

fullReport.html:

Hold the long while hourly closes stay above 30,242; a 2-close break stops the system out. But this is Day 2 into quarter-end and the jobs week, so do not add into the markup.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jun 29 map, checked on the Jun 30 session)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultMeasured
1MNQ holds above the 30,000 psych and the 29,900 AVWAP on hourly closesTRIGGERED, PLAYED OUTHeld both decisively (RTH low 30,274, far above 30,000) and trended to a 30,600 RTH high, blowing past the old +2s 30,219 and the 30,069 reference. Closed 30,501, +1.69%. Full continuation.
2MNQ loses the 29,655 ATR stop on a 2-close basisNOT TRIGGEREDNever threatened; the session low was 30,274, more than 600 points above the 29,655 stop.
3MNQ chops between the 29,655 stop and the 30,000 psych into quarter-endNOT TRIGGEREDNo chop in that zone; price held above 30,000 the entire session and trended up.
4MES holds 7,500 on hourly closes (it capped just below today)TRIGGERED, PLAYED OUTCleared and held 7,500 on every hourly close (RTH low 7,515.5), closed 7,541.75 (+0.65%) and confirmed NQ. The breakout sustained.
5MGC stays below its 4,099 stop and MCL holds its 69.44 stopTRIGGERED, MIXEDThe equities-up / gold-heavy thesis played out: gold closed 4,050.5 (-0.21%) heavy, below 4,099 on every hourly close (max close 4,081). But the crude leg whipsawed: MCL lost its long and flipped its 1hr stop short at 15:00 UTC (closed 70.04, -0.58%). Risk-on persisted equity-led; the commodity legs diverged.

fullReport.title: Tomorrow's Map (for Wednesday, July 1, 2026)
fullReport.html:
Map for Wednesday, July 1, 2026 (Q3 start). Filters: Wednesday gates out HOF and MID (both skip Wed), so only BIR and BNC are eligible. Treat the open as an event open: ADP lands 8:15 ET (pre-open) and ISM Manufacturing 10:00 ET, so the first opening-range break is suspect per the COMPASS event-open lesson. Calendar: Wed Jul 1 ADP Employment 8:15 ET and ISM Manufacturing 10:00 ET; then Thu Jul 2 June jobs report 8:30 ET with a 1:00 pm ET early close; Fri Jul 3 market CLOSED (Independence Day observed, Jul 4 Saturday). All levels from today's 1hr chart.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Day 2 risk-on. Row 1 (hold 30,000 + 29,900 AVWAP) TRIGGERED and PLAYED OUT, ran to 30,600. Row 4 (MES holds 7,500) TRIGGERED and PLAYED OUT, closed 7,542 and confirmed NQ. Row 5 (gold below 4,099 + crude holds 69.44) TRIGGERED but MIXED: gold stayed heavy below 4,099 and equities ripped as mapped, but crude flipped its stop short. Rows 2 and 3 (stop loss / chop) NOT TRIGGERED, price trended well above the 29,655 stop all day.
